findings.description: Sliding-window accumulator in moving_average subtracts the outgoing element on each step; floating-point subtraction accumulates rounding error over long sequences, silently producing incorrect averages that can drift arbitrarily far from the true window sum.

percentile materialises values with sorted() after the pct guard, so a partially-consumed generator yields a truncated ordered list and returns a silently wrong result; the Iterable[float] signature advertises generator support but the function cannot safely handle one that has already been partially read.

safe_mean materialises values with list() and is subject to the same silently-wrong-result problem as percentile when given a partially-consumed iterator; both functions share this contract gap with no documentation or guard.

StreamingMedian.add has no type guard before calling insort; passing None or a non-comparable value raises TypeError from inside bisect.insort mid-operation, and while insort is typically atomic for the common case, the failure surface is undocumented and callers receive no early, clear error.

The min(low+1, len(ordered)-1) guard in percentile is a defensive no-op when the single-element early-return on line 32 has already guaranteed len>=2 at this point; harmless but indicates incomplete reasoning about the guard's necessity.
